_meta.MANUALLY_MAINTAINED: This file is hand-edited, not auto-derived from TODO.md's prose each run. Permission state is security-relevant (it gates every GitHub API write call made on behalf of an agent — see workers/permission-guard.js) and must not depend on successfully parsing free text out of a document. When the person updates push permissions in 'TO DO LIST.docx' (General section), they must update THIS file to match as a separate, deliberate second step. (Historical note: this used to also warn that scripts/sync-todo.js does not touch this file — that script was deleted 2026-07-18 along with the docx->TODO.md flow it served; the update-this-file-deliberately rule is unchanged.)
_meta.push_semantics: push:true means an agent may write directly into that project's own repo. push:false means an agent may only research/recommend/write-to-its-own-repo — any attempted external write is redirected into office-AI-agents (this repo, under agent-output/<project-key>/) instead of being pushed out, and the redirect is logged. Pull (read-only checkout/fetch) is always allowed regardless of push, but capped at 1/day repo-wide by the same guard module (config/project-permissions.json does not carry the pull cap — it's a fixed General rule, not per-project).
_meta.office_agents_push_true_is_load_bearing: office-agents: push:true is NOT a formality — as of the 2026-07-08 config-driven-self-write session, workers/agent-runner.js's REPO_TO_PROJECT_KEY maps office-AI-agents' own repo name to this 'office-agents' key too (the old hardcoded REPO_NAME exemption was removed), so commitFileToRepo()/fileGitHubIssue() now run every self-repo write through canPushToProject('office-agents') like any other project. If this is ever flipped to false, EVERY report-writing workflow (daily/weekly summaries, incident reports, model-education digests, side-plot resolutions, the Architect's suggestion files) gets silently redirected into agent-output/office-agents/... instead of its real path — the exact class of bug this project has spent the month finding. Change this value deliberately, never as a side effect of a broader edit.
_meta.code_write_policy:
code_write:false on office-agents means neither the 11-agent simulation nor the Architect (office Agent 10, and the separate agents/architect_agent.py Smart Archive research script) have any autonomous code-writing path today — both are research/recommendation/markdown-report-and-Issue output only (see workers/permission-guard.js checkCodeWriteAllowed(), which already blocks .js/.py/.ts/... writes repo-wide unless explicitCodeTask is set). This flag documents that as a standing fact, not a switch to flip: if a future session adds any code-writing capability for the Architect or any other agent, code_write must stay a per-change decision — do NOT flip this to true as a standing permission. Any future code-writing action requires (1) explicit human approval for that specific change, given at the time, not a durable flag, and (2) a generated documentation artifact explaining the change, produced at the time of the change. See POLICY.md.

UPDATE 2026-07-11 (see code_write_model_scope_2026-07-11 below): this note and the office-agents.code_write:false flag it describes are UNCHANGED by that update — the Architect and the 11-agent simulation still have no autonomous code-writing path in THIS repo, and this project-scoped flag must still not be flipped to true as a standing permission without a fresh, explicit, dated decision. What's new is a SEPARATE, additive, model-scoped axis (the top-level `code_write` key, not this per-project one) governing code-writing directed at OTHER projects (e.g. notebook-x) by the acting model. The two axes are independent and both apply where relevant — this note's 'never a standing flag' principle still holds for office-agents and for Claude/Groq globally; it no longer holds for Gemini globally, per that dated decision.
_meta.code_write_model_scope_2026-07-11 (SUPER: Explicit, dated owner decision (2026-07-11): code-write authorization is now ALSO scoped by ACTING MODEL, applied globally across all projects (see the top-level `code_write` key below), in addition to the existing per-project push/code_write axis above — the owner's decision was explicitly model-based, not project-based, so this sits alongside rather than replaces the per-project settings. Gemini: `code_write.gemini: true` — broadly authorized to write and push code autonomously, any project. This IS a standing flag, and is a deliberate, narrow supersession of the 'code_write must stay a per-change decision, never a standing flag' principle in code_write_policy above, but ONLY for Gemini — that principle is otherwise unchanged. Matches TODO.md's own Notebook-X section ('gemini has permissions to write code in notebook-x') and the code-writing already run in practice via notebook-x-daily.mjs's housekeeping_codeAssessment()/frontend_code_change handling. Groq: `code_write.groq: false` — never writes code, any project, no exceptions, no escalation path; Groq stays scoped to routine per-case text work per config/token-economy.json. Claude: `code_write.claude: "per-change-only"` — UNCHANGED by this decision; Claude remains reviewer/manager by default (hard-case escalations, AI Search bar, Agent 10/Architect root-level changes per CLAUDE.md's token-economy section) and may write code only with explicit, per-instance human authorization given at the time of that specific change, never as a standing flag. Enforced by workers/permission-guard.js checkCodeWriteAllowed({ filePath, model }); a code-file write must clear BOTH axes (the acting model's global code_write permission here, AND the target project's own push/code_write settings above) before it's allowed.
_meta.code_write_blanket_2026-07-18: Explicit, dated owner decision (2026-07-18, Q&A-engine rebuild follow-up session): the model-scoped `code_write` map ({gemini: true, groq: false, claude: 'per-change-only'}) is RETIRED and replaced by the single blanket flag `automated_code_write: false` below — no agent or automation writes or modifies code, files, or tools of any kind, autonomously, regardless of which model is acting. Why: the 2026-07-11 Gemini standing grant existed solely to authorize notebook-x-daily.mjs's autonomous code pushes; the 2026-07-18 rebuild retired those write paths after the 2026-07-11/12 notebook_backend.py incident (and after finding the checkCodeWriteAllowedForModel() gate was never actually wired into frontend_code_change), and this session deleted notebook-x-daily.mjs itself — so a per-model write-permission matrix now had zero legitimate consumers and only preserved a standing Gemini grant nobody should exercise. Code changes are reserved for Claude Code working directly with the owner, or a future owner-directed special task to the dormant Architect persona — in both cases authorized per-change, at the time, by a human (workers/permission-guard.js checkCodeWriteAllowed()'s explicitCodeTask path), never as a standing flag. This restores code_write_policy's original 'per-change decision, never a standing flag' principle universally, ending the narrow Gemini exception. The per-project `push` flags below are UNCHANGED and still meaningful — push governs where non-code output (reports, markdown, Issues-redirects) may land, and notebook-x push:true also preserves the dormant-but-real path for an owner-directed Architect task; office_agents_push_true_is_load_bearing above still applies in full.
